data.body:
IMPACT: (1) False confidence — tests pass but behavior is untested. Example: test-merge-to-main-qt4u.sh passed for months while the v2-only patterns it checked were stale. (2) Fragile maintenance — sed sentinel ranges break on refactoring (dso-t2wb). String counts break when code moves between functions. (3) Flaky failures — timing-based behavioral proxies like the sleep-and-measure parallelism test (w20-51ti) fail under load. (4) Oscillation risk — fixing a fragile test by adjusting the grep pattern is a surface fix that doesn't address the missing behavioral coverage.

FIX NEEDED IN TWO PLACES: (1) The TDD RED phase guidance in /dso:implementation-plan and /dso:tdd-workflow needs to explicitly prohibit grep-the-source-file assertions for behavioral requirements. RED tests must exercise the code under test, not scan its text. (2) Existing change-detector tests (~70 across 15 files) should be triaged: delete tests that duplicate behavioral coverage from integration tests, replace tests for untested behavior with real integration tests, keep tests that enforce legitimate structural constraints (no relative paths, config migration, frontmatter validation).
